page: Hetzner Cloud cx43 is an x86_64 virtual server built on the QEMU hypervisor, featuring 8 shared vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC-Rome processors running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 160 GB local SSD, and the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate a mix of strong and average performance characteristics. The AMD EPYC-Rome processor achieves a strong single-core CPU performance score of 2548.38 in stress-ng tests, placing it in the top 25% of tested hardware. However, multi-core performance in stress-ng (20252.78) and Geekbench multi-core (7324.0) fall into the average tier. Memory bandwidth is measured at 888.0 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 89.3 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, both representing average tier performance. Single-threaded gzip compression is a notable bottleneck, registering in the bottom 25% at 23.39 GB/sec, while LLM inference text generation for a small 135M model is strong at 259.42 tokens/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the cx43 offers a cost-efficient entry point for multi-core workloads that do not require dedicated CPU execution. The shared CPU allocation introduces potential performance variability under sustained high-load conditions, which is a key tradeoff for the lower cost profile. With its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average database operation performance of 6708.71 in PassMark, this server is suited for web serving, software compilation, light database operations, and small-scale LLM text generation tasks.
description: Hetzner Cloud cx43 is an x86_64 virtual server utilizing the QEMU hypervisor and powered by 8 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM (1.95 GB per core) and 160 GB of local SSD storage, with no integrated GPU. Performance benchmarks show a strong single-core stress-ng score of 2548.38, while multi-core metrics, such as a Geekbench multi-core score of 7324.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 15774.82, remain in the average tier. The shared CPU allocation provides a cost-efficient resource profile, though it introduces potential performance variability. This server is suited for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and light database operations.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cx43 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 160 GB of local SSD storage, delivering balanced average-tier performance for multi-core workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cx43 is an x86_64 virtual server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 160 GB SSD storage, suited for general web serving.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors, local SSD storage, and balanced multi-core performance for budget-conscious workloads.
bullet_points: 8 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory and 160 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Shared CPU allocation offering high resource density at a lower cost tier
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and light database tasks
